content: Just as high school language arts curricula use structured reflection writing exercises to help learners correct implicit reasoning gaps and reinforce better problem-solving habits, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can borrow the structured writing workshop framework from literacy education to refine their ad-hoc self-critique processes. This means framing LLM self-reflection as a guided, multi-step writing workshop assignment rather than a free-form self-query, with explicit prompts for drafting, peer (multi-agent) review, and revision aligned to how human learners build reflective skill.
